title: gonolobus asper
intro: Gonolobus asper, a flowering plant native to Central and South America, is also known as the "rough-seeded vine." Traditionally, it has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to provide health benefits such as reducing fever, alleviating pain, and treating various infections. Gonolobus asper is often prepared as a tincture or infusion for its herbal benefits, and it is also used in some traditional remedies for its supposed medicinal properties.
